system.description:
The Shamanic Adept must follow the Shamanic tradition and choose a Totem. Unlike a full Shaman, they do not receive their Totem's modifiers for magical activities. Instead, the Totem defines the limits of what the adept can do: they may only cast spells or conjure spirits for which that Totem would normally offer modifiers.

For example, a Bear Shamanic Adept can cast Health spells and conjure Forest Spirits — but not Combat spells or City Spirits. Totems that grant no spell or spirit modifiers (e.g. Coyote) are incompatible with this path.

Shamanic Adepts have full use of Sorcery and Conjuring for defensive purposes and retain access to astral perception and projection.

SR2E p.163
